name: Tournedos of Lotte with Lobster & Lobster But
recipeIngredient: Celery stalk: 1, trimmed and coarsely chopped
Lotte (Monkfish): 18 fillets, skinned and cut into 2 oz portions
Salt and white pepper: to taste
recipeInstructions:
To make Lobster Butter:
1. Steam or boil lobsters until just tender, about 8-10 minutes.
2. Cool, then remove all lobster meat from shells and refrigerate, covered; set aside.
3. In a large saucepan, heat clarified butter over medium heat.
4. Add onion, carrot, celery, garlic, thyme, tarragon, and bay leaf; toss for 3 minutes.
5. Add lobster shells and stir for 3 minutes.
6. Add cognac and simmer gently, covered, for 2-3 hours.
7. Strain the mixture through a fine mesh sieve, pressing solids firmly. Set aside for 15 minutes.
8. Skim any froth from the surface and strain again through several layers of dampened cheesecloth, leaving behind any milky residue in the bottom of the pan (discard).
9. Cool and chill or freeze in small containers.
To prepare Tournedos:
1. Pat lotte dry and season with salt and pepper.
2. Heat clarified butter in a heavy large skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Working in batches, sauté lotte until lightly golden, about 2 minutes per side.
4. Scatter shallots around the fish and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
5. Pour cognac into the skillet and cook for 1 minute longer.
6. Transfer tournedos to warm serving plates and keep warm.
7. Add cream to the skillet and reduce by half.
8. Add remaining cream and bring to a boil, coating a spoon lightly.
9. Gradually add lobster butter, whisking constantly.
10. Strain sauce into a clean saucepan and stir in reserved lobster meat.
11. Simmer gently for 2 minutes.
12. Arrange lobster pieces between lotte fillets and serve with sauce, garnished with parsley.
